chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: سَأَلْتُهُ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ رَجُلٌ دَخَلَ دَارَ رَجُلٍ فَوَثَبَ كَلْبٌ عَلَيْهِ فِي الدَّارِ فَعَقَرَهُ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ دُعِيَ فَعَلَى أَهْلِ الدَّارِ أَرْشُ الْخَدْشِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لَمْ يُدْعَ فَدَخَلَ فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْهِمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Sheykh from the people of Al Kufa, from one of our companions,

(The narrator) says, ‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! A man enters a house of a man, so a dog leaps upon him in the house, so it injures him’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was invited, so it is upon the people of the house, the compensation of the scratch; and if he was not invited, so he entered (of his own accord), so there is nothing upon them’.
